REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO FACTS 13, 14, 15 AND 25. ALSO SEE DISPUTED FACTS IN DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF ADDITIONAL UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT NOTES THAT IT IS IN RECEIPT OF TWO SETS OF MOVING PAPERS COURTESY COPIES. BOTH ARE NOT TABBED. PARTIES FILED NO OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE. CRC 3.1354. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft And Her Attorney
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Thomas Purtell, Esq. 415-722-6291 for Plaintiff.
James O'Brien, Esq. Nicole Russell, Esq. 415-513-7942 415-513-7942 jobrien@jamesobrienlaw.com for Plaintiff

K. Douglas Atkinson, Esq. 415-362-2580 Lewis Brisbois Bisgarrd & Smith for Defendant.
Court reporter: Jeanine Farrell, CSR #7774, 415.515.3548, depos@westcoastreporters.net

ARGUED. The court raises 3 issues: relevance of trust documents, privilege log, sanctions. Mr. Purtell presents arguments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALEXA WHITE's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft And Her Attorney.

Plaintiff Alexa White's motion to compel further responses by defendant Mary Beecroft to Ms. White's first set of document requests and for monetary sanctions is denied. Ms. White must pay $900 in monetary sanctions to Ms. Beecroft no later than September 23, 2025.

This motion is a non-starter and should never have been filed. There is no basis to discover the trust documents and no privilege log is needed if no documents are not withheld on privilege grounds. Because the motion lacked substantial justification, Ms. White is required to pay the reasonable monetary sanctions requested by Ms. Beecroft.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jeanine Farrell, CSR #7774, 415.515.3548, depos@westcoastreporters.net, Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft And Her Attorney
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF ALEXA WHITE's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft And Her Attorney.

Plaintiff Alexa White's motions to compel further responses to her set two document requests from defendant Mary Beecroft, as an individual and in her capacity as a trustee, and for monetary sanctions are denied in full. Ms. Beecroft's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. Ms. White's reliance on Duncan v. Kihagi (2021) 68 Cal. App. 5th 519 to obtain discovery regarding other property owned by Ms. Beecroft is misplaced. Duncan does not establish a bright line rule that other property evidence is always admissible in a rental unit habitability case and it does not address the discoverability of such information. The facts in Duncan are on the extreme end of showing a pattern of rental unit habitability abuses and thus that case is readily distinguishable from most other cases alleging habitability abuses. The court is unaware of any case law or statutory authority establishing trial admissibility or discoverability of other property information for all habitability cases. Consequently, the court believes that discoverability of such normally private and sensitive information is discoverable only where the plaintiff makes some showing from public records or other source that the defendant has engaged in a pattern of habitability abuses. Because Ms. White has made no such showing, her motion is denied. Because Ms. White's motion does not lack substantial justification, Ms. Beecroft's request for fees is also denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
